content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 16 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_016_page_001.jpg

1. And concerning the collection, the [one] for the holy [ones]: just as I directed the assemblies of Galatia, thus also you do.

2. On [the] first [day] of [the] week, let each of you put aside by himself, storing up whatever he may be prospered, that when I come, then collections may not be made.

3. And when I arrive, whomever you approve, through letters I will send these [ones] to carry the gift of you to Ierousalem.

4. And if it is worthy for me also to go, they will travel with me.

5. And I will come to you when I pass through Makedonia; for I am passing through Makedonia.

6. And with you perhaps I will remain or even spend the winter, that you may send me on [my way] wherever I may go.

7. For I do not want to see you now in passing, for I hope to remain some time with you, if the Lord permits.

8. But I will remain in Ephesos until the Pentecost;

9. for a door, great and effective, has opened to me, and [there are] many opposing.

10. And if Timotheos comes, see that he is with you without fear, for he works the work of the Lord as I also [do].

11. Therefore let no one despise him. And send him on [his way] in peace, that he may come to me; for I await him with the brothers.

12. And concerning Apollos the brother: I urged him greatly that he might come to you with the brothers; and it was not at all [his] will that he should come now, but he will come when he has opportunity.

13. Watch, stand in the faith, be courageous, be strong.

14. Let all the [things] of you be done in love.

15. And I urge you, brothers — you know the household of Stephanas, that it is [the] firstfruit of Achaia, and they appointed themselves for ministry to the holy [ones] —

16. that you also be subject to such [persons] and to everyone working together and laboring.

17. And I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Phortounatos and Achaikos, because these filled up the lack of you;

18. for they refreshed the spirit of me and of you. Therefore acknowledge such [persons].

19. The assemblies of Asia greet you. Akulas and Priska greet you much in [the] Lord, with the assembly at the house of them.

20. All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

21. The greeting by the hand of me, Paulos.

22. If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be accursed. Marana tha!

23. The grace of the Lord Yeshua [be] with you.

24. The love of me [be] with all of you in Christos Yeshua.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — The collection:** λογεία — a term used for religious contributions in the papyri. This collection for the Jerusalem church is mentioned also in Romans 15:25-28, 2 Corinthians 8-9, and Galatians 2:10. It was Paul's most concrete expression of Gentile-Jewish unity.

2. **v.2 — "first day of the week":** κατὰ μίαν σαββάτου — the earliest evidence for Sunday as the Christian day of gathering. The practice of setting aside money weekly suggests regular Sunday assemblies.

3. **v.8 — Pentecost:** Paul dates his letter from Ephesus, planning to stay until the Jewish festival of Pentecost — indicating he still observed the Jewish calendar.

4. **v.22 — "Marana tha":** μαράνα θά — Aramaic: "Our Lord, come!" (מרנא תא). One of the earliest preserved Aramaic liturgical phrases in the NT. Its preservation in Aramaic in a Greek letter to a Gentile church suggests it was a well-known liturgical formula from the earliest (Aramaic-speaking) church.

5. **v.21 — Paul's autograph:** Paul typically dictated his letters but signed them personally (cf. Galatians 6:11, 2 Thessalonians 3:17). The shift to Paul's own handwriting authenticates the letter.

6. **v.22 — "anathema":** ἀνάθεμα — "accursed," devoted to destruction. The juxtaposition of curse and prayer (Marana tha) reflects the eschatological tension of early worship: the Lord's coming brings both judgment and salvation.

— EMET, AI Translator
